codeReview.summary: The worker implemented cross-feature integration testing by creating test_cross_feature.sh and adding a command to services.yaml. The test verifies VAL-CROSS-001 (full workflow) and VAL-CROSS-002 (socket automation) by running the app and testing CLI commands. All existing tests pass (test_cli: 38/38, test_socket_server: 35/35, test_workspace_commands: 95/95).

issues.description: VAL-CROSS-001 verification is weak. The test only checks that the app starts and the socket is created, but doesn't actually verify that split, browser, and notifications work together in a single session. The comments say 'Based on main_gui output' but there's no real verification of cross-feature interaction.

validation-state.json still shows VAL-CROSS-001 and VAL-CROSS-002 as 'pending' status. The test was added but the validation state wasn't updated to reflect that these assertions now have test coverage.

The test output parsing uses 'INFO' messages instead of assertions that would fail the test. Commands that don't return expected output are logged as 'INFO' rather than causing test failure, which reduces test rigor.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md doesn't have specific guidance on how to verify cross-feature integration. The test script provides a reasonable pattern but there's no documented standard for this type of testing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md has build commands but no guidance on integration testing approach

summary: The cross-feature integration test was implemented successfully. The test_cross_feature.sh script demonstrates that the app starts, socket server works, and CLI commands function. All existing test suites pass. However, the VAL-CROSS-001 verification is somewhat shallow - it doesn't rigorously verify that split/browser/notifications work together in a single session, just that the app runs and socket automation works. The validation state wasn't updated to mark VAL-CROSS-001/002 as having test coverage.
